questions.q: For the conversion $\mathrm{CCl}_4(\mathrm{l}) \rightarrow \mathrm{CCl}_4(\mathrm{~g})$ at 1 bar and 350 K , the correct set of thermodynamic parameters is (Boiling point of $\mathrm{CCl}_4$ is $77^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ )

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: For the following spontaneous process $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_{(\mathrm{l})} \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_{(\mathrm{s})}$ at 268 K , which of the following is true?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Three samples of 100 g of water (samples I, II and III), initially kept at 1 atm pressure and 298 K were given the following treatments.
Sample I was heated to 320 K and cooled to 298 K
Sample II was heated to 300 K , cooled to 273 K and heated to 298 K
Sample III was heated to 373 K and cooled to 298 K
At the end of these processes, the internal energy of
options: I and III are the same; II is lower than that of I and III
I, II and III are the same

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Standard molar enthalpy of formation of $\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ is equal to
options: The standard molar enthalpy of combustion of carbon (graphite)
The standard molar enthalpy of combustion of $\mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})$
The standard molar enthalpy of combustion of $\mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g})$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Hydrazine used in rocket fuels can be obtained by the reaction of ammonia and hydrogen peroxide according to the following equation
$2 \mathrm{NH}_3(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{l}) \rightarrow \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4(\mathrm{l})+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})\left(\Delta \mathrm{H}^0\right.$ reaction $\left.=-241 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol}\right)$
If $\Delta \mathrm{H}^0$ (formation) of $\mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ are $-46.1,-187.8$ and $-285.8 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$ respectively, $\Delta \mathrm{H}^0$ for the decomposition of hydrazine into $\mathrm{N}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2$ is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Which reaction is spontaneous at all temperatures at standard pressure and concentration?
options: exothermic reaction with a decrease in entropy.
exothermic reaction with an increase in entropy
endothermic reaction with a decrease in entropy
endothermic reaction with an increase in entropy

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Silanes are silicon hydrides of general formula $\mathrm{Si}_{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{H}_{2 \mathrm{n}+2}$ and have several applications. From the data given below, the bond dissociation enthalpy of $\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{Si}$ bond can be deduced as:
$\Delta \mathrm{H}$ of the reaction $2 \mathrm{Si}(\mathrm{s})+3 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{Si}_2 \mathrm{H}_6(\mathrm{~g})$ is $80.3 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
Bond dissociation enthalpy for $\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H}=436 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$
Bond dissociation enthalpy for Si-H = $304 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$
$\Delta \mathrm{f}_H[\mathrm{Si}(\mathrm{g})]=450 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: At constant volume, 6.0 mol of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and 100 kPa was heated to 250 kPa . The molar heat of $\mathrm{H}_2$ at constant pressure $\left(\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{P}}\right)=28.9 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. ( assume that the heat capacity values do not change with temperature). The final temperature of the $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas and the change in entropy of the process are

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The daily energy requirement of a teenager is 7800 kJ . As calculated from the data given in the table below, the amount of glucose he has to consume (g) per day assuming that the entire energy he requires comes from the combustion of glucose is
Molecule $\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{f}}\left(\mathrm{kJ}\mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right)$
$\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12}\mathrm{O}_6$ $-1273$
$\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ -394
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ $-286$

If the solution enthalpies of $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$are in the ratio 6:5, the enthalpy of hydration of $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$ion is

questions.q: For the reaction $4 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5(\mathrm{~g}), \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {reaction }}=-112 \mathrm{~kJ}$. If the $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is assumed to be formed in the reaction as a solid, $\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {reaction }}$ will be $\left(\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {sublimation }}\right.$ of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is $54 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ )

questions.q: At 298 K , change in internal energy for the complete combustion of fullerene, $\mathrm{C}_{60}(\mathrm{~s})$, an allotrope of carbon, and the enthalpy of formation of $\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ are $-25970 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $-393 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ respectively. The enthalpy of formation of $\mathrm{C}_{60}(\mathrm{~s})$ at 298 K is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: 2.0 L of $\mathrm{N}_2$ gas kept at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and 5 atm pressure were expanded isothermally against a constant pressure of 1 atm until the pressure of the gas reaches 1 atm . Assuming ideal behaviour, reversible work of expansion in this process ( in J ) is close to

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The work done (kJ) in the irreversible isothermal compression of 2.0 moles of an ideal gas from 1 bar to 100 bar at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ at constant external pressure of 500 bar is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The normal boiling point and $\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {vap }}$ of a liquid ' X ' are 400 K and $40 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ respectively. Assuming $\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {vap }}$ to be constant, which of the following is correct?
I. $\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {vap }}>100 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ at 400 K and 0.5 atm
II. $\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {vap }} < 100 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ at 400 K and 1 atm
III. $\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {vap }} < 100 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ at 400 K and 2 atm
IV. $\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {vap }}=100 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ at 400 K and 1 atm

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The change in internal energy $(\Delta \mathrm{U})$ for the reaction $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Br}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{HBr}(l)$ when 2.0 moles each of $\mathrm{Br}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ react is
$\left(\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Br}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{HBr}(\mathrm{~g}) ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {reaction }}=-109 \mathrm{~kJ} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {vap }} \text { of } \mathrm{HBr}=213 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: 2.0 moles of an ideal gas expands isothermally $\left(27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$ and reversibly from a pressure of 1 bar to 10 bar. The heaviest mass that can be lifted through a height of 10 m by the work of this expansion is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: At 298 K , the standard free energies of formation of cis- and trans-1,2-dichloroethene are 41.549 kJ and 33.235 kJ respectively. The most appropriate mol ratio of trans- and cis- isomers at equilibrium at 298 K is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Following is the $P$ vs $V$ plot of a cyclic process $1 \rightarrow 2,2 \rightarrow 3,3 \rightarrow 4,4 \rightarrow 1$, denoted as I, II, III and IV respectively for a system of one mole of an ideal gas. Assume that there is heat exchange between the system and surroundings only in II and IV. Which of the following is/are correct?
options: In II and IV $\Delta \mathrm{S}$ is zero
In I and III, $\Delta \mathrm{S}$ is zero
I and III are isothermal and reversible
In II and IV, change in internal energy of the gas $(\Delta \mathrm{U})$ is zero

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
From the following data
$\begin{aligned}
& 2 \mathrm{CH}_6 \mathrm{~N}_2(\ell)+5 \mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{~N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{~g})\left(\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}^0=-2606 \mathrm{~kJ}\right) \text { and } \\
& \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\ell) \rightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{~g})\left(\Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{v}}^0=44 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)
\end{aligned}$
Heat of combustion of $\mathrm{CH}_6 \mathrm{~N}_2$ at 298 K can be calculated as

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Heat of reaction and heat of formation will be the same in
I. $\quad 1 / 2 \mathrm{~N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$
II. $\quad \mathrm{Xe}(\mathrm{g})+2 \mathrm{~F}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{XeF}_4(\mathrm{~g})$
III. $\mathrm{N}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_3(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_3(\mathrm{~g})$
IV. $\mathrm{C}($ diamond $)+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Which of the following properties may have positive values of $\Delta \mathrm{H}$ ?
(i) Lattice enthalpy
(ii) Hydration enthalpy

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The figure represents the processes $\mathrm{AB}, \mathrm{BC}$ and CA undertaken by a certain mass of an ideal gas. Along the path AB , the gas is isothermally compressed with release of 800 J heat to the surroundings. It is then compressed adiabatically along the path BC and the work done is 500 J . The gas then returns to the state A along path CA and absorbs 100 J heat from the surroundings. The work done by the gas along the path CA is:

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A student adds ' $x$ ' $g$ of iron $(\mathrm{Fe})$ powder to dil. HCl and measures the work done by the reaction between HCl and the added Fe to be 1000 J . If the experiment was conducted at a constant pressure of 1 atm at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, mass of Fe powder added is:
